stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The associated tags (transformers, llama, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern AI/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of Llama-3.1 indicates ongoing development and community interest in Meta's latest large language models.

stories.title: Anthropic is paying $15 billion a year for access to Elon Musk’s data centers
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Anthropic is reportedly paying a massive $15 billion annually for compute resources.
The compute access is provided through SpaceX's Colossus data centers in Memphis, TN.
The details of this major partnership were revealed in SpaceX's S-1 IPO filing.

stories.title: Meta lays off thousands of employees to offset AI investments
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Meta is conducting significant layoffs to fund its substantial investments in Artificial Intelligence.
The company frames the headcount reduction as a necessary measure to improve operational efficiency.
This move highlights the current industry trend of balancing massive AI R&D spending with cost-cutting measures.

stories.title: Google publishes exploit code threatening millions of Chromium users
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Google has released exploit code targeting Chromium users.
The vulnerability was reportedly disclosed 29 months prior to the code's publication.
The associated security flaw has since been patched by Google.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local, on-device operation.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI agents privately on the user's own hardware.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Famously secret about its finances, SpaceX opens its books for the first time
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX is publicly disclosing financial information for the first time, a significant shift for the company.
The company claims to have identified the 'largest TAM in human history,' suggesting massive future market potential.
The story addresses a major change in transparency for a highly secretive and influential aerospace/tech firm.

stories.title: FatGid: FreeBSD 14.x kernel local privilege escalation
stories.author: WhyNotHugo
bullet_points: A local privilege escalation vulnerability was discovered in the FreeBSD 14.x kernel.
The vulnerability is named 'FatGid' and affects the kernel.
This is a critical security flaw relevant to system administrators and security researchers.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It indicates the model is being used for text-generation tasks.
The associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports integration with various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code with open-source models via Ollama.
It bridges the gap between proprietary models (Anthropic) and the open-source ecosystem (Ollama).

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within the user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: I can’t believe how fast Google vibe coded my first Android app
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: The article highlights the rapid development of Android applications using Google's AI coding tools.
The author claims to have built multiple functional apps in a single afternoon with minimal manual coding.
This suggests a significant acceleration in the barrier to entry for mobile app development via AI assistance.

stories.title: ‘Fuck you, Bambu’: How one private message could change the face of 3D printing
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Bambu Lab, a leading 3D printing manufacturer, is facing community backlash.
The controversy stems from a private message from Bambu Lab to a user on Reddit demanding the deletion of their code.
The 3D printing community is rallying to support the affected user against the company.

stories.title: Leaving the V8 in the past: The all-electric Mercedes-AMG GT 4-Door
stories.author: Michael Teo Van Runkle
bullet_points: Mercedes-AMG is transitioning its high-performance GT line to an all-electric powertrain.
The electric model demonstrates impressive performance metrics, including a fast 0-60 mph time.
The efficiency (miles/kWh) of the new electric AMG GT is highlighted as a significant achievement.

stories.title: Variance Reduction for Expectations with Diffusion Teachers
stories.author: Jonathan Lorraine
bullet_points: The research addresses high variance in Monte Carlo expectations used when training downstream pipelines (e.g., text-to-3D) guided by frozen diffusion models.
The core contribution is CARV, a compute-aware variance-accounting framework designed to reduce the computational cost associated with noisy gradient estimation.
This work is relevant to efficient deployment and fine-tuning of large generative models by improving the stability and speed of gradient-based training.

stories.title: Equilibrium Reasoners: Learning Attractors Enables Scalable Reasoning
stories.author: Zico Kolter
bullet_points: Introduces 'Equilibrium Reasoners' (EqR), a new framework for scalable reasoning in AI.
Hypothesizes that generalizable reasoning stems from learning 'task-conditioned attractors' in latent dynamical systems.
Addresses the current gap in understanding how iterative, test-time compute models generalize beyond memorization.

stories.title: EvoStruct: Bridging Evolutionary and Structural Priors for Antibody CDR Design via Protein Language Model Adaptation
stories.author: Murray Patterson
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical limitation in current GNN methods for antibody CDR design: severe vocabulary collapse and poor residue prediction.
EvoStruct proposes bridging evolutionary and structural priors by adapting a Protein Language Model to overcome the limitations of de novo learning from structural data.
The work highlights the importance of incorporating evolutionary substitution patterns, which current GNN encoders are failing to capture effectively.

stories.title: AiraXiv: An AI-Driven Open-Access Platform for Human and AI Scientists
stories.author: Yue Zhang
bullet_points: Proposes 'AiraXiv,' an AI-driven open-access platform designed to handle the surge in human and AI-generated research.
Addresses scalability issues in traditional academic publishing due to rising submission volumes and reviewer workload.
Introduces a new publishing paradigm where both human and AI entities can participate as authors and readers.

stories.title: Agent JIT Compilation for Latency-Optimizing Web Agent Planning and Scheduling
stories.author: Christos Kozyrakis
bullet_points: Addresses high latency and error rates in current Computer-Use Agent (CUA) systems that rely on sequential LLM calls.
Introduces Agent Just-In-Time (JIT) compilation as a novel method to optimize web agent planning and scheduling.
Aims to improve the efficiency of agents that automate complex, natural language tasks (e.g., ordering items online).

stories.title: You Only Need Minimal RLVR Training: Extrapolating LLMs via Rank-1 Trajectories
stories.author: Yu Meng
bullet_points: The research investigates the underlying geometry of parameter trajectories when using Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) to improve LLMs.
The study demonstrates that RLVR weight trajectories are extremely low-rank and highly predictable.
A rank-1 approximation of the parameter deltas is sufficient to capture the majority of downstream performance gains in LLMs.

stories.title: DelTA: Discriminative Token Credit Assignment for Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: Introduces 'DelTA,' a method to analyze Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).
Addresses the gap in understanding how response-level rewards affect token-level probability changes in LLMs.
Proposes that the policy-gradient update implicitly functions as a linear discriminator over token-gradient vectors.

stories.title: Google officially announces that ads will be included in AI Mode search results
stories.author: sofumel
bullet_points: Google is integrating advertisements directly into its new AI Mode search results.
This signals a significant shift in how Google monetizes its generative AI features.
The move suggests that advertising will become a core component of the AI search experience.

stories.title: Show HN: Rmux – A programmable terminal multiplexer with a Playwright-style SDK
stories.author: shideneyu
bullet_points: RMux is a new, custom-built terminal multiplexer written in Rust, designed to address automation limitations in tools like tmux.
It features a dual interface: a tmux-compatible CLI and a typed, asynchronous Rust SDK.
The core innovation is providing 'Playwright-style' automation capabilities (structured snapshots, locator-style waits) for terminal sessions.

stories.title: Chromium publishes fixed exploit 4 years later, turns out it's actually unfixed
stories.author: infosec.exchange via hugoarnal
bullet_points: Chromium released a security fix for an exploit that was reportedly patched years ago.
The published fix was later found to be ineffective or incomplete.
This highlights potential issues in the security patching and disclosure lifecycle of major browser projects.

stories.title: XSS Is Deadly for Passkeys: The Hidden Risk of Attestation None
stories.author: scotthelme.co.uk via fazalmajid
bullet_points: The article discusses a critical security vulnerability related to Passkeys.
The specific risk highlighted is that Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) can be dangerous when 'Attestation None' is used.
This points to a nuanced, technical security concern within modern authentication methods.
